title: ericameria parryi var. imula
intro: Ericameria parryi var. imula, also known as a shrub-form of the Parry goldenrod, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils. The health benefits of Ericameria parryi var. imula include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture or tea, and can also be applied topically as a salve or poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
